bids.reasoning: At the opening (t=0.0), I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because there is no opponent history yet and no time pressure to concede. I therefore choose the top value on each issue, especially holding firm on issueC and issueD because they carry the highest importance in my preferences.

At t=0.02 it is far too early to concede much, and the opponent’s offer is very low for my preferences, especially on high-importance issueC and issueD. Their message is just pressure and is not supported by meaningful concession behavior, so I will keep my high-value positions while making a tiny concession on low-weight issueA to avoid simply repeating my opening bid.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04 and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer for me rather than showing real concession. Their threats to walk are cheap talk and not supported by time pressure, so I will make only a small concession on a lower-weight issue while holding firm on my most important issues, especially issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the same low-utility offer despite threatening messages. I will make a small concession on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueC and issueD, which matter most to my utility.

I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on low-weight issueA, since it is still very early at t=0.08 and there is no need to move toward the opponent’s low-utility repeated demand yet. The opponent has not conceded at all and their threats look like cheap talk, so I will hold firm on my most important issues, especially issueC and issueD, while avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid, conceding mainly on the low-weight issues A and B while holding firm on high-value issueC and issueD. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their latest valid offer did move issueD to my preferred valueC, suggesting some room for trade; I will reciprocate on A/B without giving up the critical issueC.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong bid around 0.73 utility rather than accepting the opponent’s low offer. The opponent has held firm on issueA/issueB/issueC but conceded issueD to my preferred valueC, so I will keep issueD and make only a small concession on my most important issueC from valueC to valueD while leaving low-weight issues at their preferred pattern.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while maintaining concessions on the lower-weight issues A and B. The opponent has held firm on issueA/valueA, issueB/valueA, and issueC/valueB, while conceding issueD to valueC; their threats look like cheap talk given the early time and repeated pattern. I will keep issueC at my top value because it dominates my utility, while offering their apparent preferences on A and B and the already-common valueC on D.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid around 0.725 by conceding issueA and issueB, which are low-weight for me, while holding issueD at my best value and only slightly relaxing issueC from valueC to valueD. At t=0.16 it is still early, and the opponent has only meaningfully conceded on issueD while repeatedly demanding issueC: valueB, which is too costly on my most important issue; their “last move” threats are cheap talk contradicted by the early time.

I am targeting a high but still conciliatory utility around 0.725 by conceding fully on the lower-weight issues A and B and keeping issueD at the opponent’s recent conceded value, while holding issueC near my top preference because it carries the most importance. At t=0.18 there is still little deadline pressure, and the opponent’s threats are cheap talk; their actual offers show only a concession on issueD while they remain fixed on issueC=valueB, which is too costly for me this early.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.74 by conceding on the low-weight issues A and B while holding firm on issueC, which is my dominant issue. At t=0.20 there is still time, and the opponent’s threats are cheap talk; their offers show they strongly prefer issueA/issueB as valueA and keep pushing issueC away from my preferred values, but they have previously offered issueD: valueC. I will therefore keep issueC at valueC and offer concessions elsewhere rather than drop to their very low-utility package.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.725 by conceding the low-weight issues A and B while preserving high value on issueD and making only a small concession on the most important issueC from valueC to valueD. At t=0.22 it is still early, and the opponent’s threats are cheap talk; their offers have mostly held firm or worsened for me, so I should not concede issueC down to valueB/valueA yet.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 because it is early-mid negotiation and the opponent’s threats are cheap talk rather than evidence of a real deadline. Their offers have mostly stayed very poor for me and even worsened at t=0.24, but they consistently push issueA/issueB to valueA and issueC away from my ideal, so I concede the low-weight A/B while holding firm on high-weight issueC and making a small adjustment on issueD to avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a strong but somewhat more conciliatory bid by keeping issueC near my top preference (my highest-weight issue) while matching the opponent’s latest movement on issueD and already conceding issueA/issueB to valueA. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I should not accept their low issueC position; their messages are mostly threats, but their offers show some flexibility on issueD and little real concession on issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.705 by fully conceding the low-weight issues A and B while holding issueC at valueD, which is almost as good as my top value and much better than the opponent’s preferred valueB/valueA. At t=0.28 there is not enough deadline pressure to accept their repeated low-utility package, and their threats to walk have not matched their continued bidding; however, their movement on issueD suggests valueD there may be acceptable to them.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.725 by conceding fully on the lower-weight issueA and issueB while keeping issueC near my top value because it is by far my most important issue. At t=0.30 there is still time, and the opponent’s threats look like cheap talk since they have continued bidding despite repeated “last offer” claims; their offers remain very poor for me and have not improved on issueC. I will match their preferred low values on issueA/issueB and keep issueD at a value they have offered before, while using issueC=valueD as my main compromise.

I am targeting a strong utility bid around 0.705, conceding fully on the lower-weight issueA and issueB while preserving most value on the critical issueC with valueD. At t=0.32 there is still time, so I should not accept the opponent’s low-utility threats at face value; their actual offers show some movement on issueD but continued pressure on issueC, so I will compromise on issueD while holding issueC near my best values.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility around 0.725 by keeping firm on issueC, which is my most important issue, while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB fully to valueA. At t=0.34 there is still time, and the opponent’s threats are cheap talk; their offers show they strongly push issueC downward but have moved somewhat on issueD, so I will offer issueD: valueC while holding issueC at valueD.

I am targeting a strong but more realistic utility by conceding the low-weight issues A and B to the opponent’s repeated preferred values while holding firm on issueC and asking for issueD=valueC. At t=0.36 there is still time, and despite repeated threats in messages, the actual offers show movement: the opponent’s latest bid finally conceded issueC=valueC, so I will build on that rather than accept their low issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 by conceding the low-weight issues issueA and issueB to valueA and offering issueD as valueD, which the opponent has previously proposed. At t=0.38 there is still time, so I should not collapse to their low-utility offers; their repeated threats are cheap talk, while their offers show they strongly prefer issueA/issueB at valueA and have sometimes tolerated better terms on issueD. I will hold firm on issueC, my highest-weight issue, at valueC.

At t=0.40 this is still mid-negotiation, so I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid by conceding on the low-weight issues issueA and issueB while holding value on the critical issueC and important issueD. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk and contradicted by continued bidding; their offers mostly demand issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA, so I concede those, but their offers to me remain too low to accept.

I am targeting a utility around 0.72 by keeping the most important issueC at my top value while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB to valueA, which the opponent has repeatedly demanded. At t=0.42 this is still mid-negotiation, so I should not accept their much lower offers or threats at face value, but I can make a meaningful concession on issueD by offering valueD instead of valueC.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility while preserving the opponent’s clear priorities on issueA and issueB, where they have repeatedly pushed valueA. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their latest valid offer significantly improved for me by conceding issueC and issueD to my top values, so I will only seek a small gain on a lower-weight issue rather than reopen the most important issues.

I am targeting a strong but plausible utility around 0.797, preserving my most important gains on issueC and issueD while conceding issueB fully. At t=0.46 there is moderate time pressure, and the opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their offers show they have recently conceded issueC and issueD to my preferred values while mostly holding firm on issueA/issueB; I will ask only a small improvement on issueA, which they have offered once before.

I am targeting a strong weighted utility of about 0.74 by conceding the low-weight issues A and B to valueA while holding firm on the important issueC and issueD at valueC. At t=0.48 there is some mid-game pressure to make a credible compromise, but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest lower-utility regression on issueC. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk; their actual offers at t=0.44 and t=0.46 show they may tolerate issueC: valueC and issueD: valueC if they get issueA/issueB at valueA.
